title: Leucanthemopsis pectinata
taxonomy: Leucanthemopsis pectinata is classified within the family Plantaginaceae. However, some sources may still consider it part of the family Scrophulariaceae. Further classification places the species within the order Lamiales. It belongs to the class Liliopsida or Magnoliopsida, although the latter classification is more commonly used. The species is also a part of the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Leucanthemopsis pectinata, commonly known as the Alpine Daisy, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Leucanthemopsis pectinata is used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach ulcers, as well as to reduce inflammation and relieve respiratory problems. The plant's antiseptic and antimicrobial properties also make it useful for wound healing and skin infections. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of Leucanthemopsis pectinata and to develop its use in modern medicine.

intro: Leucanthemopsis pectinata, also known as alpine daisy, is a perennial plant native to the high mountain regions of the Mediterranean. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Leucanthemopsis pectinata include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to alleviate respiratory symptoms and soothe digestive issues.
